page.seoDescription: Practical articles about website development, SEO, performance and digital growth for Indian businesses.
page.title: Clear guidance for better digital decisions.
page.description: Research-led articles for business owners planning websites, search strategies and technology investments.

posts.title: How Much Does a Website Cost in India?
posts.seoTitle: Website Development Cost in India: A Practical Guide
posts.description: A practical guide to website budgets, cost drivers and questions to ask before requesting proposals.
posts.imageAlt: Website design and budget planning workspace with a laptop and analytics
posts.author: CodingPara editorial team
posts.introduction: Website pricing depends on what the website must achieve, how much original content it needs and which systems it must connect to. A useful estimate starts with scope rather than a fixed package price.

sections.heading: Start with the business goal
paragraphs: Define the action visitors should take and the outcome the business expects. A brochure site, lead-generation website, online store and customer portal require very different levels of planning and engineering.
Clear goals help agencies recommend only the pages, journeys and integrations that support the investment.

sections.heading: Understand the real cost drivers
paragraphs: Design depth, page count, copywriting, product data, payment flows, third-party integrations and migration risk all affect the final scope.
Internal review cycles and incomplete content can also increase delivery time, so ownership and approvals should be agreed before work begins.

sections.heading: Prioritise foundations before features
paragraphs: Responsive design, accessibility, performance, analytics and technical SEO usually create more long-term value than decorative effects.
Launch with a strong foundation and add lower-priority features after real customer behaviour can be measured.

sections.heading: Plan ownership and ongoing improvement
paragraphs: Confirm who owns the domain, hosting, source code, design files and analytics accounts. The proposal should also explain maintenance, security updates and support after launch.
Reserve budget for useful content and measured improvements so the website continues to support the business.

faqs.q: Is there one standard website price?
faqs.a: No. Price depends on scope, content, design, integrations and the level of custom development required.

faqs.q: How should I compare website proposals?
faqs.a: Compare assumptions, deliverables, exclusions, ownership, testing and post-launch support—not only the total price.

posts.title: SEO Checklist for Small Businesses
posts.seoTitle: Small Business SEO Checklist: Technical, Local and Content
posts.description: Build a sound search foundation with a prioritised checklist covering technical, local and content SEO.
posts.imageAlt: SEO analytics dashboard with search, local map and checklist elements
posts.author: CodingPara editorial team
posts.introduction: Small-business SEO works best when the essentials are handled consistently. Use this checklist to fix discoverability, strengthen local relevance and publish content that answers real customer questions.

sections.heading: Make every important page discoverable
paragraphs: Ensure search engines can crawl the site, important pages return a successful status and the XML sitemap contains only canonical URLs.
Connect Google Search Console and review indexing reports regularly instead of assuming every page appears in search.

sections.heading: Optimise pages around search intent
paragraphs: Give each page one clear purpose. Write a descriptive title, a useful main heading and copy that directly answers the need behind the search.
Use internal links to connect related services, locations and educational articles without forcing keywords into every sentence.

sections.heading: Strengthen local search signals
paragraphs: Complete the Google Business Profile with accurate categories, hours, services, photos and contact details. Keep the business name, address and phone details consistent across trusted listings.
Ask genuine customers for reviews and respond helpfully; never manufacture ratings.

sections.heading: Measure enquiries, not rankings alone
paragraphs: Track calls, forms and other meaningful actions alongside organic traffic. Rankings can indicate visibility, but qualified enquiries reveal whether the strategy supports the business.
Review results by landing page and search theme, then improve pages that attract attention without generating action.

faqs.q: How quickly can a small business see SEO results?
faqs.a: Technical changes may be detected quickly, while meaningful growth in competitive searches commonly takes several months.

faqs.q: Does every small business need a blog?
faqs.a: No. Publish only when articles answer valuable customer questions that service or product pages cannot cover well.

posts.title: React vs WordPress for Business Websites
posts.seoTitle: React vs WordPress: Which Is Better for a Business Website?
posts.description: Compare editing, performance, flexibility and ownership before choosing your next website platform.
posts.imageAlt: Balanced comparison of code components and a visual content editor
posts.author: CodingPara editorial team
posts.introduction: React and WordPress solve different problems. The better choice depends on who updates the site, how custom the experience must be and what technical support is available after launch.

sections.heading: Choose around the publishing workflow
paragraphs: WordPress provides a familiar content-management experience and can suit teams that publish frequently without developer support.
A React or Next.js site can also use a content system, but that workflow must be selected and integrated intentionally.

sections.heading: Compare flexibility and complexity
paragraphs: React is well suited to highly interactive interfaces, custom applications and experiences that must integrate deeply with other services.
WordPress can deliver many standard business and commerce requirements efficiently, although excessive plugins and page-builder layers can make maintenance harder.

sections.heading: Treat performance as an implementation outcome
paragraphs: Neither platform is automatically fast. Image handling, scripts, hosting, caching and engineering decisions determine the customer experience.
Set measurable performance requirements and test representative pages on real mobile connections.

sections.heading: Plan long-term ownership
paragraphs: Consider updates, security, hosting, developer availability and content ownership over several years—not only launch speed.
Choose the simplest platform that reliably supports the required customer journey and your team's ability to operate it.

faqs.q: Is React always faster than WordPress?
faqs.a: No. Either can be fast or slow depending on architecture, hosting, media, scripts and implementation quality.

faqs.q: Can WordPress support a custom design?
faqs.a: Yes. A custom theme and well-planned blocks can provide a distinctive design without relying on a heavy page builder.
